mnemonics.title: IE Register Bits
mnemonics.content:
E-A--S-ET1-EX1-ET0-EX0
(Everyone Ate Some Eggs, Eat One More)

mnemonics.title: IP Register Bits
mnemonics.content:
P-S--PT1-PX1-PT0-PX0
(Priority Set: Priority Timer 1, Priority External 1, Timer 0, External 0)

mnemonics.title: Enable Sequence
mnemonics.content:
EA First - Enable All before enabling individual interrupts
(EA = Enable All bits)

mnemonics.title: ISR Golden Rules
mnemonics.content:
Keep ISR Short - Save Context - Clear Flags - Restore Context
(K-S-C-R)

acronyms.usage: Controls timer and external interrupt types (88H)
Global interrupt enable bit in IE register
Bit to enable timer interrupts (ET0, ET1)
Bit to enable external interrupts (EX0, EX1)
Bit to select level or edge triggering (IT0, IT1)
Flag indicating external interrupt occurred (IE0, IE1)

visualTips.visualization: Draw a flowchart: Main Program → Interrupt Request → Complete Instruction → Save Context → ISR Execution → Restore Context → Main Program. Show decision diamond for priority check.
8-bit box: [EA][--][--][ES][ET1][EX1][ET0][EX0]. Fill each with 1 when enabling. Remember bits 5 and 4 are undefined/unused.
8-bit box: [--][--][--][PS][PT1][PX1][PT0][PX0]. Set bit to 1 for high priority. Default is low (0).
Memory map showing: 0000H (Reset), 0003H (INT0), 000BH (T0), 0013H (INT1), 001BH (T1), 0023H (Serial). Each is 8 bytes apart.
Stack diagram: Low Priority ISR interrupted by High Priority ISR. Draw nested boxes showing preempted execution.

analogies.analogy: Interrupt is like a phone ringing when someone calls you (you stop what you're doing to answer), while polling is like repeatedly asking 'Any calls?' every few seconds (wasteful, inefficient).
ISR is like a fire alarm - when it sounds, everyone knows there's an emergency (interrupt), people follow evacuation procedure (ISR execution), then return to work.
Priority is like hospital triage - a critical patient (high priority interrupt) gets treated before someone with a minor injury (low priority).

analogies.analization: Vector table is like an address book - each person's name (interrupt source) has their address (ISR location) so you know where to go when contacted.

analogies.analogy: EA (Enable All) is like the main switch at home - individual circuit breakers (EX0, ET0, etc.) only work when main switch is ON.
